steps.title: Understand the passage's principle about stronger winds
steps.content: The passage explains that when actual winds are stronger than forecast, the predicted maximum generation is too low—meaning actual generation exceeds predicted. The graph's title confirms it measures exactly this scenario: additional electricity when winds were stronger than forecast.

steps.title: Read the graph data and match to the correct choice
steps.content:
The West region bar shows approximately 150 thousand additional MWh—the amount generated beyond the forecast.

Therefore the winds were “strong enough to generate about 150 thousand more MWh.” Choices B and C incorrectly describe weak winds (the opposite of what the data represents), and choice D uses the wrong value.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
